key_insight: A scrutable interface can make generative AI more useful for K-12 teachers by letting them steer outputs through an editable knowledge representation rather than opaque prompt tweaking.
engineering_relevance: For educational engineering workflows, Concept Catalyst shows how human-in-the-loop interface design can improve content generation, user trust, and practical adoption of AI tools.
method: Designed Concept Catalyst, a scrutable interface for teacher-facing generative AI
Used Wizard-of-Oz testing with middle and high school engineering teachers
Collected depth interviews plus screen/audio recordings and generated classroom content
evidence_signal: An exploratory study with 10 teacher interviews found that the interface helped structure AI use and supported reflection, efficacy, efficiency, and motivation.
watch_item: Results come from a small Wizard-of-Oz study, so real-world performance with fully automated models and broader teacher populations still needs validation.
paper_title: Concept Catalyst: Exploring Scrutable Interfaces to Structure K-12 Teacher Interactions with Generative AI
